demographics.living_situation: lives in a group residential home with 24-hour disability support staff

contact_nok.relationship: disability support worker / group home staff

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: This is the support worker at his group home — we need help now. He's been smashing his head against the window over and over and he's biting anyone who gets near him. He's got a big lump on his forehead already and there's broken glass everywhere. We can't calm him down and we can't safely give him his tablet.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He's been getting worse for about three hours since his dinner routine got changed, and in the last hour nothing we normally do is working — we're scared he's going to seriously hurt himself or one of us.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about three hours ago, not long after his usual evening meal routine was unexpectedly changed
symptom_narrative.duration: about three hours and escalating, much worse in the last hour
symptom_narrative.character: began with repetitive hand-wringing and loud noises, then he started banging his head hard against the wall and window and biting himself and staff
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst we've seen him — he will not stop and he's already hurt his head
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: he keeps hitting the front of his head; he's biting his own arms and lunging at staff
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting more intense, no let-up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — our usual de-escalation, quiet space and distraction aren't working
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any change to his routine sets him off, and people coming near him makes it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he's pacing non-stop, breathing fast, red in the face and making constant distressed noises
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has behaviours like this sometimes when his routine is disrupted, but it has never escalated this badly or this long before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he can walk around and normally needs one-to-one help for everything; right now he can't be approached or cared for safely

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: profound autism spectrum disorder, diagnosed in early childhood
past_medical_history.how_managed: 24-hour supported accommodation with one-to-one support and a behaviour support plan

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: profound difficulty communicating — he is non-verbal and cannot tell us when he is in pain or what he needs

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: olanzapine wafer/tablet kept as an as-needed calming medication

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a staffed group home; two support workers present; strongly reliant on predictable routines and visual supports

objective_data_offered.value: a large lump and fresh grazes on the middle of his forehead where he has been hitting his head
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seen briefly on video from a distance; staff cannot get close enough to examine it

objective_data_offered.value: he is pacing constantly, breathing fast, looks flushed and sweaty, and is making continuous loud distressed noises
broken glass from a shattered picture frame is on the floor; doors have been locked to stop him running off
his usual as-needed olanzapine tablet is on hand but staff cannot safely get him to take it by mouth because he is biting
